guidelines.title: Antidepressant Medication Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
ANTIDEPRESSANT MEDICATION DOSING GUIDELINES

SSRIs (Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors):

Sertraline (Zoloft):
- Initial: 50 mg daily
- Titration: Increase by 50 mg every 1-2 weeks
- Target: 50-200 mg daily
- Maximum: 200 mg/day
- First-line for depression, anxiety, PTSD, OCD

Escitalopram (Lexapro):
- Initial: 10 mg daily
- Target: 10-20 mg daily
- Maximum: 20 mg/day
- Most selective SSRI, good tolerability
- Elderly: Max 10 mg/day

Fluoxetine (Prozac):
- Initial: 20 mg daily (morning)
- Target: 20-80 mg daily
- Maximum: 80 mg/day
- Longest half-life (1-3 days active metabolite 4-16 days)
- Good for tapering/missed doses

Paroxetine (Paxil):
- Initial: 20 mg daily
- Target: 20-50 mg daily
- Maximum: 50 mg/day (60 mg for OCD)
- Most anticholinergic SSRI, weight gain
- Worst for discontinuation syndrome

Citalopram (Celexa):
- Initial: 20 mg daily
- Target: 20-40 mg daily
- Maximum: 40 mg/day (20 mg if >60 years, hepatic impairment)
- QTc prolongation risk at higher doses

Fluvoxamine (Luvox):
- Initial: 50 mg at bedtime
- Target: 100-300 mg daily (divided if >100 mg)
- Maximum: 300 mg/day
- Primarily for OCD

SNRIs (Serotonin-Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibitors):

Venlafaxine (Effexor XR):
- Initial: 37.5-75 mg daily
- Titration: Increase by 75 mg every 4-7 days
- Target: 75-225 mg daily
- Maximum: 375 mg/day
- BP monitoring at higher doses
- Severe discontinuation syndrome

Duloxetine (Cymbalta):
- Initial: 30 mg daily x1 week, then 60 mg
- Target: 60 mg daily
- Maximum: 120 mg/day
- Also for neuropathic pain, fibromyalgia
- Avoid with hepatic impairment

Desvenlafaxine (Pristiq):
- Initial/Target: 50 mg daily
- Maximum: 100 mg/day (limited additional benefit >50 mg)
- Active metabolite of venlafaxine

Levomilnacipran (Fetzima):
- Initial: 20 mg daily x2 days
- Titration: Increase to 40 mg, then by 40 mg every 2 days
- Target: 40-120 mg daily
- Maximum: 120 mg/day

ATYPICAL ANTIDEPRESSANTS:

Bupropion (Wellbutrin):
- IR: Start 100 mg twice daily, target 100 mg TID
- SR: Start 150 mg daily, target 150 mg twice daily
- XL: Start 150 mg daily, target 300 mg daily
- Maximum: 450 mg/day
- No sexual dysfunction, weight neutral
- Contraindicated: Seizure disorder, eating disorders

Mirtazapine (Remeron):
- Initial: 15 mg at bedtime
- Target: 15-45 mg at bedtime
- Maximum: 45 mg/day
- Paradoxically less sedating at higher doses
- Weight gain, good for anorexia/insomnia

Trazodone:
- Depression: 150-600 mg/day in divided doses
- Insomnia (off-label): 25-100 mg at bedtime
- Maximum: 600 mg/day
- Orthostatic hypotension, priapism risk

Vilazodone (Viibryd):
- Initial: 10 mg daily x7 days
- Then 20 mg daily x7 days
- Target: 20-40 mg daily with food
- Maximum: 40 mg/day

Vortioxetine (Trintellix):
- Initial: 10 mg daily
- Target: 10-20 mg daily
- Maximum: 20 mg/day
- Cognitive benefits, less sexual dysfunction

TRICYCLIC ANTIDEPRESSANTS (TCAs):

Amitriptyline:
- Initial: 25-50 mg at bedtime
- Titration: Increase by 25 mg every 3-7 days
- Target: 150-300 mg/day
- Therapeutic level: 80-200 ng/mL (with nortriptyline)
- Highly anticholinergic, cardiac risk

Nortriptyline (Pamelor):
- Initial: 25 mg at bedtime
- Target: 50-150 mg/day
- Therapeutic level: 50-150 ng/mL
- Less anticholinergic than amitriptyline

MAOIs (Reserved for treatment-resistant):

Phenelzine (Nardil):
- Initial: 15 mg three times daily
- Target: 60-90 mg/day
- Dietary tyramine restrictions essential

Transylcypromine (Parnate):
- Initial: 10 mg twice daily
- Target: 30-60 mg/day
- 14-day washout from SSRIs (5 weeks for fluoxetine)

Selegiline transdermal (EMSAM):
- 6 mg/24h patch: No dietary restrictions
- 9 or 12 mg/24h: Dietary restrictions apply

AUGMENTATION STRATEGIES:

- Aripiprazole 2-15 mg/day (FDA approved)
- Quetiapine XR 150-300 mg/day (FDA approved)
- Olanzapine + fluoxetine (Symbyax)
- Lithium 300-600 mg/day (level 0.4-0.8)
- Thyroid hormone (T3) 25-50 mcg/day
- Bupropion addition to SSRI

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (APA Practice Guidelines)

guidelines.title: Antipsychotic Medication Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
ANTIPSYCHOTIC MEDICATION DOSING GUIDELINES

SECOND-GENERATION (ATYPICAL) ANTIPSYCHOTICS:

Risperidone (Risperdal):
- Schizophrenia: Start 1-2 mg/day, target 4-8 mg/day
- Bipolar mania: 2-3 mg/day
- Maximum: 16 mg/day
- EPS increases significantly >6 mg
- Long-acting injectable: 25-50 mg IM every 2 weeks

Paliperidone (Invega):
- Oral: Start 6 mg daily, target 3-12 mg daily
- Invega Sustenna (monthly): 234 mg day 1, 156 mg day 8, then 117 mg monthly
- Invega Trinza (every 3 months): After stable on Sustenna
- Active metabolite of risperidone

Olanzapine (Zyprexa):
- Schizophrenia: Start 5-10 mg/day, target 10-20 mg/day
- Bipolar: 10-15 mg/day
- Maximum: 20 mg/day
- High metabolic risk (weight gain, diabetes)
- IM (acute agitation): 10 mg, may repeat x2 (max 30 mg/day)

Quetiapine (Seroquel):
- Schizophrenia: Start 25 mg BID, titrate to 300-800 mg/day
- Bipolar depression: Start 50 mg at bedtime, target 300 mg
- Bipolar mania: Target 400-800 mg/day
- Maximum: 800 mg/day
- XR formulation for once daily dosing

Aripiprazole (Abilify):
- Schizophrenia: Start 10-15 mg/day, target 10-30 mg/day
- Bipolar: 15-30 mg/day
- Adjunct depression: 2-15 mg/day
- Maximum: 30 mg/day
- Maintena (LAI): 400 mg monthly (or 300 mg)
- Aristada (LAI): 441-882 mg monthly
- Lower metabolic risk, akathisia common

Ziprasidone (Geodon):
- Start 20 mg twice daily with food (essential for absorption)
- Target 40-80 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 160 mg/day
- QTc prolongation - avoid with other QT-prolonging drugs
- Weight neutral
- IM: 10-20 mg (max 40 mg/day)

Lurasidone (Latuda):
- Schizophrenia: Start 40 mg/day, target 40-160 mg/day
- Bipolar depression: Start 20 mg/day, target 20-120 mg/day
- Maximum: 160 mg/day
- MUST take with food (≥350 calories)
- Lower metabolic risk, akathisia

Clozapine (Clozaril):
- Treatment-resistant schizophrenia only
- Start 12.5 mg once or twice daily
- Titrate slowly: 25-50 mg/day every 1-2 weeks
- Target: 300-450 mg/day (divided doses)
- Maximum: 900 mg/day
- Therapeutic level: 350-600 ng/mL
- REMS program for agranulocytosis monitoring
- ANC monitoring weekly x6 months, then biweekly x6 months, then monthly

Asenapine (Saphris):
- 5-10 mg sublingual twice daily
- Must dissolve under tongue, no eating/drinking x10 min
- Maximum: 20 mg/day

Iloperidone (Fanapt):
- Titration: 1 mg BID day 1, increase by 2 mg/day
- Target: 6-12 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 24 mg/day
- CYP2D6 poor metabolizers: Reduce dose by 50%

Cariprazine (Vraylar):
- Schizophrenia: 1.5-6 mg/day
- Bipolar mania: 3-6 mg/day
- Bipolar depression: 1.5-3 mg/day
- Very long half-life (active metabolite 1-3 weeks)

Brexipiprazole (Rexulti):
- Schizophrenia: Start 1 mg/day, target 2-4 mg/day
- Adjunct depression: 0.5-3 mg/day
- Maximum: 4 mg/day

FIRST-GENERATION (TYPICAL) ANTIPSYCHOTICS:

Haloperidol (Haldol):
- Acute psychosis: 2-5 mg PO/IM q4-8h
- Maintenance: 5-20 mg/day
- IV: 2-5 mg (continuous EKG monitoring for QTc)
- Decanoate: 10-20x daily oral dose, monthly
- High EPS risk

Fluphenazine:
- Oral: 2.5-10 mg/day in divided doses
- Decanoate: 12.5-25 mg IM every 2-4 weeks

Chlorpromazine (Thorazine):
- 200-800 mg/day in divided doses
- Highly sedating, orthostatic hypotension

ACUTE AGITATION MANAGEMENT:

Intramuscular options:
- Haloperidol 5 mg + Lorazepam 2 mg + Diphenhydramine 50 mg (B52)
- Olanzapine 10 mg IM (avoid with benzodiazepines)
- Ziprasidone 20 mg IM
- Aripiprazole 9.75 mg IM

METABOLIC MONITORING:
- Weight: Baseline, then monthly x3 months, then quarterly
- Fasting glucose/lipids: Baseline, 3 months, then annually
- Blood pressure: Each visit
- Waist circumference: Annually

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (APA Schizophrenia Guidelines)

guidelines.title: Anxiolytic and Sedative-Hypnotic Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
ANXIOLYTIC AND SEDATIVE-HYPNOTIC DOSING GUIDELINES

BENZODIAZEPINES:

Short-Acting:

Alprazolam (Xanax):
- Anxiety: Start 0.25-0.5 mg TID, target 0.5-4 mg/day
- Panic disorder: Start 0.5 mg TID, target 1-10 mg/day
- Maximum: 10 mg/day (rarely needed)
- XR: 0.5-1 mg daily, increase by 1 mg every 3-4 days
- High addiction potential, difficult to taper

Lorazepam (Ativan):
- Anxiety: 1-3 mg/day in 2-3 divided doses
- Acute agitation: 1-2 mg IM/IV
- Maximum: 10 mg/day
- No active metabolites (preferred in elderly, liver disease)
- Half-life: 10-20 hours

Intermediate-Acting:

Clonazepam (Klonopin):
- Panic disorder: Start 0.25 mg BID, target 1-4 mg/day
- Anxiety: 0.5-2 mg/day in divided doses
- Maximum: 20 mg/day (rarely >4 mg needed)
- Longer half-life, easier tapering

Long-Acting:

Diazepam (Valium):
- Anxiety: 2-10 mg 2-4 times daily
- Muscle relaxant: 2-10 mg TID-QID
- Alcohol withdrawal: 10-20 mg q1-2h until calm (CIWA protocol)
- Maximum: 40 mg/day for anxiety
- Very long half-life (active metabolites 20-100 hours)

Chlordiazepoxide (Librium):
- Alcohol withdrawal: 50-100 mg q6h day 1, taper over 3-5 days
- Anxiety: 5-25 mg TID-QID

BENZODIAZEPINE EQUIVALENCE (approximate):
- Alprazolam 0.5 mg = Lorazepam 1 mg = Clonazepam 0.5 mg = Diazepam 10 mg

BENZODIAZEPINE TAPERING:
- Reduce by 10-25% every 1-2 weeks
- Slower taper for long-term use (>6 months)
- Consider switch to long-acting before taper
- May take 4-6 months for full taper

NON-BENZODIAZEPINE ANXIOLYTICS:

Buspirone (Buspar):
- Initial: 7.5 mg twice daily
- Titration: Increase by 5 mg every 2-3 days
- Target: 15-30 mg twice daily
- Maximum: 60 mg/day
- Takes 2-4 weeks for effect
- No sedation, no dependence, no withdrawal
- Not effective for acute anxiety or panic

Hydroxyzine (Vistaril/Atarax):
- 25-100 mg up to QID
- Antihistamine with anxiolytic properties
- Can cause sedation, anticholinergic effects

SEDATIVE-HYPNOTICS FOR INSOMNIA:

Z-Drugs (Non-benzodiazepine receptor agonists):

Zolpidem (Ambien):
- IR: 5 mg (women), 5-10 mg (men) at bedtime
- CR: 6.25 mg (women), 6.25-12.5 mg (men)
- Maximum: 10 mg (IR), 12.5 mg (CR)
- Take immediately before bed
- Complex sleep behaviors risk

Zaleplon (Sonata):
- 5-20 mg at bedtime
- Ultra-short acting (1 hour half-life)
- Can take middle of night if ≥4 hours to wake time

Eszopiclone (Lunesta):
- Start 1 mg at bedtime
- Target: 2-3 mg at bedtime
- Maximum: 3 mg/night
- Only Z-drug with no time limit on use
- Metallic taste common

MELATONIN RECEPTOR AGONISTS:

Ramelteon (Rozerem):
- 8 mg within 30 min of bedtime
- No controlled substance classification
- For sleep-onset insomnia

ORYXIN RECEPTOR ANTAGONISTS:

Suvorexant (Belsomra):
- Start 10 mg within 30 min of bedtime
- May increase to 20 mg
- Maximum: 20 mg/night

Lemborexant (Dayvigo):
- 5 mg at bedtime, may increase to 10 mg
- For sleep-onset and/or sleep maintenance

OTHER SEDATIVES:

Trazodone (off-label insomnia):
- 25-100 mg at bedtime
- Start low in elderly (12.5-25 mg)
- Orthostatic hypotension risk

Doxepin (Silenor):
- 3-6 mg at bedtime
- FDA-approved for sleep maintenance
- Low-dose TCA mechanism

Mirtazapine (off-label):
- 7.5-15 mg at bedtime
- More sedating at lower doses (paradoxically)

GABAA MODULATORS:

Gabapentin:
- Anxiety (off-label): 300-600 mg TID
- Insomnia: 300-600 mg at bedtime
- Titrate slowly to avoid sedation

Pregabalin (Lyrica):
- Anxiety (off-label): Start 75 mg BID, target 150-300 mg BID
- Maximum: 600 mg/day
- Scheduled (CV) due to abuse potential

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (APA guidelines, AASM guidelines)
